session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9372 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the provided arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Classify PR review feedback by filling in semantic fields from mechanical CLI output. 16→ 17→## Steps 18→ 19→1. **Run mechanical classification:** 20→ 21→ ```bash 22→ erk exec classify-pr-feedback [--pr <number>] [--include-resolved] 23→ ``` 24→ 25→ Pass `--pr <number>` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. Pass `--include-resolved` if specified in `$ARGUMENTS`. 26→ 27→ This returns JSON with mechanically pre-classified items: 28→ - **review_submissions**: PR-level reviews (classification: "actionable" | "informational" | "needs_llm") 29→ - **review_threads**: Inline review threads (pre_existing_candidate flag set for bot + restructured) 30→ - **discussion_comments**: Discussion comments (classification: "informational" | "needs_llm")
... [169 lines omitted] ...
104→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 106→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 144→ "error": null 159→## Error Case 173→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 191→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll run the mechanical classification command as specified in the skill, passing the `--pr 9372` argument:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run PR feedback classifier for PR 9372

Claim (line 86): "Extracts plan number from `P{number}-*` branch names"

session.assistant: Now I need to fill in the semantic fields for the items that need LLM judgment. Let me examine the PR details and classify the feedback:
session.tool_result:
{"body":"## Summary

This commit completes Phase 4 of the naming migration by systematically renaming plan-specific identifiers to PR-focused terminology throughout the codebase. The changes clarify that "plans" are fundamentally PR-based artifacts and improve consistency with GitHub's PR model.

## Key Changes

- Renamed `has_plan_title_prefix()` to `has_pr_title_prefix()` in CLI constants and all call sites
- Renamed `maybe_update_plan_dispatch_metadata()` to `maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata()` in metadata helpers and all imports
- Renamed CLI flags: `--from-plan-file` → `--from-pr-file`, `--keep-plan-file` → `--keep-pr-file`, `--copy-plan` → `--copy-pr`
- Renamed internal parameters: `is_plan_derived` → `is_pr_derived`, `from_plan_file` → `from_pr_file`, `copy_plan` → `copy_pr`
- Updated help text and docstrings to use "PR" terminology instead of "plan"
- Renamed enum constant `SortKey.PLAN_ID` → `SortKey.PR_ID` in TUI sorting logic

<details>
<summary>Files Changed</summary>

### Modified (32 files)

- `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` - Updated command documentation tables
- `docs/learned/architecture/unified-dispatch-pattern.md` - Updated function name references
- `docs/learned/cli/workflow-commands.md` - Updated metadata helper documentation
- `docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md` - Updated code examples and imports
- `docs/learned/integrations/github-review-decision.md` - Table alignment fix
- `docs/learned/planning/index.md` - Updated documentation index
- `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md` - Updated function names in patterns
- `docs/learned/planning/one-shot-workflow.md` - Updated function references
- `docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md` - Updated tripwire descriptions
- `docs/learned/tui/status-indicators.md` - Table alignment fix
- `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/plan_workflow.py` - Renamed `_has_plan_title_prefix()` to `_has_pr_title_prefix()`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/create_impl_context_from_plan.py` - Updated parameter documentation
- `src/erk/cli/commands/implement.py` - Updated function calls
- `src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py` - Updated import and function call
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot/cli.py` - Updated example text
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` - Updated function calls
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/duplicate_check_cmd.py` - Updated function calls
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list/cli.py` - Renamed `SortKey.PLAN_ID` to `SortKey.PR_ID`
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list/operation.py` - Updated sort key references
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py` - Renamed function and updated docstrings
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/cli.py` - Renamed internal functions, updated help text
- `src/erk/cli/commands/wt/create_cmd.py` - Renamed flags and parameters throughout
- `src/erk/cli/constants.py` - Renamed function and removed unused constant
- `src/erk/tui/sorting/logic.py` - Updated sort key references
- `src/erk/tui/sorting/types.py` - Renamed enum and updated comments
- `src/erk/tui/widgets/status_bar.py` - Updated comment
- `tests/commands/management/test_impl.py` - Updated test names and assertions
- `tests/commands/pr/test_view.py` - Updated test assertions
- `tests/commands/test_create_copy_impl.py` - Updated test names and docstrings
- `tests/commands/test_ensure_worktree.py` - Updated function calls
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_naming_and_validation.py` - Updated comments
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_output_modes.py` - Updated test names
- `tests/commands/workspace/create/test_plan_file_operations.py` - Renamed all test functions and assertions
- `tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/test_metadata_helpers.py` - Updated imports and test names
- `tests/unit/cli/test_constants.py` - Updated function names and test names

</details>

","title":"Phase 4: Rename plan-related CLI flags, constants, and help text to PR terminology","url":"https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9372"}
session.assistant: Now let me get the full text of the review threads to accurately classify them:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: gh pr view 9372 --comments | grep -A 20 "Audit PR Docs" | head -40
Get full audit PR docs comments

session.tool_result:
## ❌ Audit PR Docs
**Last updated:** 2026-03-21 09:29:40 PT
Found 3 violations across 2 files. Inline comments posted for each.
<details> <summary>Details</summary>
### Patterns Checked ✅ Verbatim code blocks — None found (template blocks in remote-workflow-template.md are TEMPLATE classification, not VERBATIM) ❌ Inaccurate claims — Found in docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md and docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md ✅ Duplicative sections — None found ✅ Drift risk (symbol renames) — PR correctly updates all symbol references
### Violations Summary - `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md:43`: Set-based field validation claim does not match implementation (no such validation exists); also references `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` (should be `resolve_pr_number_for_branch()`) - `docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md:9`: Tripwire says "silent skip with warning" but function silently returns with no warning; also `write_dispatch_metadata()` described as calling `get_plan()`/`PlanNotFound` (actually `get_managed_pr()`/`PrNotFound`) - `docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md:81`: Pre-existing: line 86 claims "Extracts plan number from `P{number}-*` branch names" — legacy format, current code uses `plnd/` prefix
### Files Reviewed
session.assistant: Let me get the specific inline thread comments:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: gh api repos/dagster-io/erk/pulls/9372/comments --paginate -q '.[] | select(.body | contains("Audit PR Docs")) | {thread_id: .id, path: .path, line: .line, body: .body}' 2>/dev/null | head -200

session.tool_result:
{"body":"**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "It validates all required fields (`schema_version`, `created_at`, `created_by`) using set difference rather than checking a single field."
Reality: The current implementation of `maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata()` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py` does NOT perform set-based field validation. It only does two LBYL early returns: one for `resolve_pr_number_for_branch()` returning None, and one for `get_workflow_run_node_id()` returning None. There is no required-fields validation using set difference, and no dim-styled warning for incomplete plan-headers.

Also, line 35 of this doc references `resolve_plan_id_for_branch()` — the actual function called is `resolve_pr_number_for_branch()`.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py:85-103`

Suggested fix: Remove the "Key Insight: Set-Based Field Validation" section as it describes behavior that does not exist. Update the bullet list to match the actual two-guard implementation.","line":43,"path":"docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md","thread_id":2969776925}
{"body":"**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: `write_dispatch_metadata() raises on error. maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata() uses LBYL guards and silent skip with warning.`
Reality: The 'with warning' description is inaccurate — `maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata()` silently returns (no warning) when preconditions fail. The only output it produces is a success message (`✓ Updated dispatch metadata on PR #...`) when it successfully writes, not a warning on skip.

Also, the description at line 25 says `write_dispatch_metadata()` calls `get_plan() — raises if PlanNotFound`. The actual code calls `get_managed_pr()` and checks `PrNotFound` (not `PlanNotFound`).

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py:24-57` (write_dispatch_metadata) and `:60-103` (maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata)","line":9,"path":"docs/learned/planning/metadata-update-patterns.md","thread_id":2969777115}
{"body":"**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected (pre-existing, surfaced on full-doc audit).

Claim (line 86): "Extracts plan number from `P{number}-*` branch names"
Reality: `maybe_update_pr_dispatch_metadata()` calls `ctx.plan_backend.resolve_pr_number_for_branch()` — which uses the `plnd/` prefix convention, not the legacy `P{number}-*` format. The P-prefix naming has been removed.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py:85`

Suggested fix: Update line 86 to: "Checks branch name via `resolve_pr_number_for_branch()` — skips gracefully if branch is not a plan branch"","line":81,"path":"docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md","thread_id":2969777287}
session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me analyze and classify these items according to the skill's requirements, then produce the final JSON output.
Based on my analysis of PR 9372:
**Review Threads Analysis:** 1. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc515QjZ** (remote-workflow-template.md:81) - Bot comment about "pre-existing" code (P{number} format) that no longer applies. This is marked as pre_existing candidate but the bot review indicates it's pre-existing and not specific to this PR's changes. Complexity: pre_existing.
2. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc515QhV** (metadata-update-patterns.md:9) - Specific inaccurate claims about function behavior and error types. This was surfaced by the PR changes but requires corrections. Complexity: single_file (one doc file).
3. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc515Qey** (metadata-update-patterns.md:43) - Inaccurate claim about set-based validation that doesn't exist. Also references an old function name. Complexity: single_file (same file as above).
**Discussion Comments:** - `4103629413` (submission-queued) - Metadata block comment, informational - `4103630269` (workflow-started) - Already classified as informational (bot action) - `4103669590` (code simplifier) - Already classified as informational - `4103670078` (tripwires) - Classified as needs_llm but says "Found 0 violations", so it's informational - `4103670211` (dignified python) - Already classified as informational - `4103670314` (test coverage) - Already classified as informational - `4103673763` (audit pr docs) - Already classified as informational
Now I'll generate the complete JSON output:

also fix function name from resolve_plan_id_for_branch() to resolve_pr_number_for_branch()", "complexity": "single_file", "original_comment": "Audit PR Docs: Inaccurate claim detected.\n\nClaim: \"It validates all required fields (`schema_version`, `created_at`, `created_by`) using set difference rather than checking a single field.\"" }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc515QjZ", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md", "line": 81, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": true, "action_summary": "Pre-existing legacy code reference: update line 86 to remove P{number}-* format reference and note plnd/ prefix convention used by resolve_pr_number_for_branch()", "complexity": "pre_existing", "original_comment": "Audit PR Docs: Inaccurate claim detected (pre-existing, surfaced on full-doc audit).\n\nClaim (line 86): \"Extracts plan number from `P{number}-*` branch names\"" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 8, "batches": [ { "name": "Pre-Existing (Auto-Resolve)", "complexity": "pre_existing", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [2] }, { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0, 1] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
